# Exodus Chapter 33 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 103–104; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 76–77 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_033_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_033_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_033_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_033_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## The Command to Depart: YHWH Will Not Go Up in Their Midst (vv. 1–6)

**1** And YHWH spoke [**vayedabber YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**]: "Go [**lekh**], go up [**aleh**] from here [**mizzeh**], you [**attah**] and the people [**veha'am**] whom [**asher**] you brought up [**he'eleyta**] from the land of [**me'erets**] Egypt [**Mitsrayim**], to [**el**] the land [**ha'arets**] which [**asher**] I swore [**nishbati**] to Abraham [**le'Avraham**], to Isaac [**leYitschaq**], and to Jacob [**uleYa'aqov**], saying [**le'mor**]: 'To your seed [**lezar'akha**] I will give it [**ettenennah**].'

**2** And I will send [**veshalachti**] before you [**lefaneykha**] an angel [**mal'akh**], and I will drive out [**vegerashti**] the Canaanite [**et-haKena'ani**], the Amorite [**ha'Emori**], and the Hittite [**vehaChitti**], and the Perizzite [**vehaPerizzi**], the Hivite [**haChivvi**], and the Jebusite [**vehaYevusi**] —

**3** to [**el**] a land [**erets**] flowing [**zavat**] with milk [**chalav**] and honey [**udevash**]; for [**ki**] I will not go up [**lo e'eleh**] in your midst [**beqirbekha**], for [**ki**] you are a stiff-necked [**qesheh-oref**] people [**am**], lest [**pen**] I consume you [**akhelkha**] on the way [**badderekh**]."

**4** And the people heard [**vayyishma ha'am**] this [**et**] evil [**hara**] word [**haddavar**], and they mourned [**vayyit'abbalu**]; and no [**velo**] man [**ish**] put [**shat**] his ornaments [**edyo**] upon him [**alav**].

**5** And YHWH said [**vayyomer YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**]: "Say [**emor**] to [**el**] the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**]: 'You [**attem**] are a stiff-necked [**qesheh-oref**] people [**am**]. One moment [**rega echad**] I could go up [**e'eleh**] in your midst [**beqirbekha**] and I would consume you [**vekhillitikha**]. And now [**ve'attah**], take down [**hored**] your ornaments [**edyekha**] from upon you [**me'aleykha**], and I will know [**ve'ed'ah**] what [**mah**] I shall do [**e'eseh**] to you [**lakh**].' "

**6** And the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**] stripped themselves [**vayyitnatslu**] of their ornaments [**et-edyam**] from Mount [**mehar**] Horeb [**Chorev**].

---

## The Tent of Meeting Outside the Camp (vv. 7–11)

**7** And Moses [**uMosheh**] would take [**yiqqach**] the tent [**et-ha'ohel**] and pitch [**venatah**] it [**lo**] outside [**michutz**] the camp [**lammachaneh**], far off [**harcheq**] from [**min**] the camp [**hammachaneh**], and he called it [**veqara lo**] the Tent of [**ohel**] Meeting [**mo'ed**]. And it was [**vehayah**] that everyone [**kol**] who sought [**mevaqesh**] YHWH [**YHWH**] would go out [**yetse**] to [**el**] the Tent of [**ohel**] Meeting [**mo'ed**] which [**asher**] was outside [**michutz**] the camp [**lammachaneh**].

**8** And it was [**vehayah**], when Moses went out [**ketset Mosheh**] to [**el**] the tent [**ha'ohel**], all [**kol**] the people would rise [**yaqumu**] and stand [**venitsvu**], each [**ish**] at the entrance of [**petach**] his tent [**oholo**], and they would gaze [**vehibbitu**] after [**acharey**] Moses [**Mosheh**] until [**ad**] he entered [**bo'o**] the tent [**ha'ohelah**].

**9** And it was [**vehayah**], when Moses entered [**kevo Mosheh**] the tent [**ha'ohelah**], the pillar of [**ammud**] cloud [**he'anan**] would descend [**yered**] and stand [**ve'amad**] at the entrance of [**petach**] the tent [**ha'ohel**], and He would speak [**vedibber**] with [**im**] Moses [**Mosheh**].

**10** And all [**vekhol**] the people would see [**ra'ah**] the pillar of [**ammud**] cloud [**he'anan**] standing [**omed**] at the entrance of [**petach**] the tent [**ha'ohel**], and all [**vekhol**] the people would rise [**qam**] and bow down [**vehishtachavu**], each [**ish**] at the entrance of [**petach**] his tent [**oholo**].

**11** And YHWH spoke [**vedibber YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**] face [**panim**] to [**el**] face [**panim**], as [**ka'asher**] a man [**ish**] speaks [**yedabber**] to [**el**] his companion [**re'ehu**]. And he would return [**veshav**] to [**el**] the camp [**hammachaneh**], and his servant [**umeshareto**] Joshua [**Yehoshua**] son of [**bin**] Nun [**Nun**], a young man [**na'ar**], would not depart [**lo yamish**] from within [**mittokh**] the tent [**ha'ohel**].

---

## Moses Entreats YHWH: Show Me Your Way (vv. 12–17)

**12** And Moses said [**vayyomer Mosheh**] to [**el**] YHWH [**YHWH**]: "See [**re'eh**], You [**attah**] say [**omer**] to me [**elay**]: 'Bring up [**ha'al**] this [**et-ha'am hazzeh**] people,' and You [**ve'attah**] have not made known to me [**lo hoda'tani**] whom [**et asher**] You will send [**tishlach**] with me [**immi**]. And yet [**ve'attah**] You [**attah**] have said [**amarta**]: 'I know you [**yeda'tikha**] by name [**beshem**], and also [**vegam**] you have found [**matsata**] favor [**chen**] in My eyes [**be'eynay**].'

**13** And now [**ve'attah**], if [**im**] indeed [**na**] I have found [**matsati**] favor [**chen**] in Your eyes [**be'eynekha**], make known [**hodi'eni**] to me [**na**] Your way [**et-derakhekha**], and I will know You [**ve'eda'akha**], so that [**lema'an**] I may find [**emtsa**] favor [**chen**] in Your eyes [**be'eynekha**]. And consider [**ure'eh**] that [**ki**] this [**hazzeh**] nation [**haggoy**] is Your people [**ammekha**]."

**14** And He said [**vayyomar**]: "My presence [**panay**] will go [**yelekhu**], and I will give you rest [**vahaniychoti lakh**]."

**15** And he said [**vayyomer**] to Him [**elav**]: "If [**im**] Your presence [**panekha**] is not [**eyn**] going [**holkhim**], do not bring us up [**al-ta'alenu**] from here [**mizzeh**].

**16** And how [**uvammeh**] then [**efo**] shall it be known [**yivvade'a**] that [**ki**] I have found favor [**matsati chen**] in Your eyes [**be'eynekha**], I [**ani**] and Your people [**ve'ammekha**]? Is it not [**halo**] in Your going [**belekhtekha**] with us [**immanu**], and we shall be distinguished [**venifleinu**], I [**ani**] and Your people [**ve'ammekha**], from every [**mikkol**] people [**ha'am**] that [**asher**] is on [**al**] the face of [**peney**] the ground [**ha'adamah**]?"

**17** And YHWH said [**vayyomer YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**]: "Also [**gam**] this [**et-haddavar hazzeh**] thing [**asher**] that you have spoken [**dibbarta**] I will do [**e'eseh**], for [**ki**] you have found [**matsata**] favor [**chen**] in My eyes [**be'eynay**], and I know you [**va'eda'akha**] by name [**beshem**]."

---

## The Glory Request: Show Me Your Glory (vv. 18–23)

**18** And he said [**vayyomar**]: "Show me [**har'eni**], I pray [**na**], Your glory [**et-kevodekha**]."

**19** And He said [**vayyomer**]: "I [**ani**] will cause all [**kol**] My goodness [**tuvi**] to pass [**a'avir**] before your face [**al-panekha**], and I will proclaim [**veqara'ti**] the name of [**beshem**] YHWH [**YHWH**] before you [**lefanekha**]; and I will be gracious [**vechanoti**] to whom [**et-asher**] I will be gracious [**achon**], and I will show mercy [**verichamti**] to whom [**et-asher**] I will show mercy [**arachem**]."

**20** And He said [**vayyomer**]: "You are not able [**lo tukhal**] to see [**lir'ot**] My face [**et-panay**], for [**ki**] no [**lo**] man [**ha'adam**] shall see Me [**yir'ani**] and live [**vachai**]."

**21** And YHWH said [**vayyomer YHWH**]: "Behold [**hinneh**], there is a place [**maqom**] with Me [**itti**], and you shall stand [**venitsavta**] upon [**al**] the rock [**hattsur**].

**22** And it shall be [**vehayah**], while My glory [**kevodi**] passes by [**ba'avor**], that I will put you [**vesamtikha**] in the cleft of [**beniqrat**] the rock [**hattsur**], and I will cover [**vesakoti**] My palm [**kappi**] over you [**aleykha**] until [**ad**] I have passed by [**ovri**].

**23** And I will remove [**vahasirotiy**] My palm [**et-kappi**], and you shall see [**vera'ita**] My back [**et-achoray**]; but My face [**ufanay**] shall not [**lo**] be seen [**yera'u**]."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

1. **Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 103–104:** The text of Exodus 33 begins in the left column of folio 103r, continuing from the closing verses of chapter 32. The parashah break (setumah) is visible before v. 1. The Masoretic accentuation and vowel pointing are clearly preserved throughout. A marginal masorah parva note appears beside v. 11 (the face-to-face passage). The chapter continues onto folio 104r, with the glory-request passage (vv. 18–23) in the central column.

2. **Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 76–77:** The Greek text of Exodus 33 (LXX) appears in the third column of page 76, continuing to page 77. The uncial script is continuous (scriptio continua) without word division. The Vaticanus text closely follows the MT verse structure with minor expansions. Notable: in v. 13 the LXX adds emphasis with "emfanison moi seauton" (manifest yourself to me), reflecting a theological expansion beyond the Hebrew.

### Key Translation Decisions

3. **v. 2 — Ethnic list order:** The MT lists six peoples (Canaanite, Amorite, Hittite, Perizzite, Hivite, Jebusite). The LXX (Vaticanus) preserves the same six with minor orthographic variation. This translation follows the MT order exactly.

4. **v. 3 — "zavat chalav udevash" (flowing with milk and honey):** Rendered with the participial form preserved: "a land flowing with milk and honey." The LXX reads "gen reousan gala kai meli," an exact functional equivalent.

5. **v. 5 — "rega echad" (one moment):** A temporal expression conveying suddenness. Rendered formally as "one moment" rather than a dynamic paraphrase. The LXX reads "mian horan" (one hour), a slight temporal expansion.

6. **v. 6 — "mehar Chorev" (from Mount Horeb):** The MT reads "from Mount Horeb" (מֵהַ֥ר חוֹרֵֽב), indicating the location where the stripping of ornaments occurred or from which point it was effective. The LXX reads similarly: "apo tou orous Choreb." This is not "from Mount Horeb onward" (a temporal reading) but a locative designation — they stripped their ornaments at Horeb.

7. **v. 7 — Habitual/iterative verbs:** Verses 7–11 employ a distinctive series of imperfect (yiqtol) forms with waw-consecutive indicating habitual or repeated action: "would take," "would rise," "would gaze," "would descend," "would speak." This iterative aspect is preserved in the translation with "would" phrasing to distinguish it from the simple narrative past of surrounding chapters.

8. **v. 11 — "panim el panim" (face to face):** The central theological statement of the chapter. Rendered literally as "face to face." The LXX reads "enopios enopio" (face to face), confirming the directness of the expression. The apparent tension with v. 20 ("you are not able to see My face") is a deliberate theological paradox in the text, not an editorial contradiction — v. 11 describes relational intimacy, while v. 20 concerns the unveiled divine glory (kavod).

9. **v. 14 — "panay yelekhu" (My face/presence will go):** The Hebrew "panim" here functions as a metonym for the divine presence itself. Rendered "My presence will go" to preserve the connection to the face-to-face motif (v. 11) while conveying the meaning. The LXX reads "autos proporeusomai sou" (I myself will go before you), interpreting the "face" as YHWH himself — a significant theological clarification.

10. **v. 16 — "venifleinu" (and we shall be distinguished):** From the root פלה (palah), meaning to be set apart, made distinct, or made wonderful. This is the same root used in Exodus 8:18 [Heb. 8:22] for YHWH's distinguishing of Goshen. Rendered "we shall be distinguished" to preserve the formal sense of separation and uniqueness.

11. **v. 19 — Double idem construction:** "vechanoti et-asher achon, verichamti et-asher arachem" — "I will be gracious to whom I will be gracious, and I will show mercy to whom I will show mercy." This is the idem per idem (same by same) construction, a Hebrew idiom expressing divine sovereignty and freedom. The doubling is preserved exactly. The LXX renders: "eleeso hon an eleo, kai oiktireso hon an oiktiro" — preserving the structure with the subjunctive (an + subjunctive), expressing conditionality.

12. **v. 20 — "lo yir'ani ha'adam vachai" (no man shall see Me and live):** The waw in "vachai" is interpreted as waw-consecutive: "and live" (i.e., and continue living), not "while alive." This is the traditional and syntactically natural reading. The LXX reads "ou dunasetai anthropos idein to prosopon mou kai zesai" (no man is able to see My face and live), adding "to prosopon" (the face) — an interpretive gloss clarifying the specific sense.

13. **v. 22 — "beniqrat hattsur" (in the cleft of the rock):** The noun "niqrah" (cleft, fissure) appears only here in the Hebrew Bible in this construct form. Rendered literally as "the cleft of the rock." The LXX reads "open tes petras" (hole of the rock), using a different spatial metaphor.

14. **v. 22 — "vesakoti kappi" (and I will cover My palm):** The Hebrew "kap" (כַּף) specifically denotes the palm or hollow of the hand, not the hand generally (yad). This distinction is preserved: "My palm" rather than "My hand." The LXX reads "te cheiri mou" (with My hand), losing the anatomical precision.

15. **v. 23 — "et-achoray" (My back):** Literally "My back parts" or "what is behind Me." This is the counterpart to "panim" (face) and denotes the aftermath or trailing manifestation of the divine glory rather than a literal anatomical reference. Rendered formally as "My back." The LXX reads "ta opiso mou" (the things behind Me), preserving the spatial metaphor.

### Theological Architecture of the Chapter

16. **The Presence Crisis:** Exodus 33 stands as the theological pivot of the golden calf narrative (chs. 32–34). The central question is not whether Israel will reach the promised land — that is affirmed in v. 1–2 — but whether YHWH himself will go with them (v. 3). The chapter moves through three stages: withdrawal of presence (vv. 1–6), mediated presence at the tent (vv. 7–11), and the negotiation for restored presence (vv. 12–23).

17. **The Panim Wordplay:** The word "panim" (face/presence) appears in seven distinct uses across the chapter: the face-to-face dialogue (v. 11), the divine presence/face going with them (vv. 14–15), passing before Moses's face (v. 19), the inability to see the divine face (v. 20), and the final statement that the face shall not be seen (v. 23). This sevenfold occurrence creates a literary architecture around the paradox of divine proximity and inaccessibility.

18. **Formal Equivalence Methodology:** This translation maintains word-order fidelity to the Hebrew wherever English syntax permits, preserves all conjunctions (waw-consecutive, waw-copulative), retains the Hebrew clause structure including fronted subjects and objects, and renders each Hebrew lexeme with a consistent English gloss throughout the chapter. Interpretive smoothing is minimized in favor of transparency to the source text.
